title: axonopus digitatus
intro: Axonopus digitatus, also known as finger grass or finger papyrus, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Axonopus digitatus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Axonopus digitatus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and poultices, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
